anthology-000:
Before this study you answered an interview question.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

Answer: I am 50 years old, I am from Hayward, CA and went to school in Sacramento, CA.

The experience of taking the SAT tests was something that I would never forget. I was nervous and excited, which led me to forget some important things I needed to be successful in life. It all started when I went to San Francisco, CA to take the SAT test.

As soon as I started taking the test I knew I was in trouble. I completely blanked out and my mind went blank. I couldn’t remember my name and what my purpose was. I left the test thinking I did horrible, so I prayed to the Lord that I wouldn’t fail the test. I prayed that I would pass the test so I could continue on to my goals and dreams. The whole way home I was nervous and in fear that I was going to fail the test.

When I got home I told my father that I had a bad day. I told him about my problems, which led him to ask me what I did on the test. I told him I couldn’t answer any questions. He then told me he was going to pray for me. He prayed that I wouldn’t fail the test. I remember thinking to myself, if I fail the test I won’t be able to go to college and I won’t be able to follow my dreams.

The next morning I went to the letterbox and I was surprised to see a letter from my high school. It was my SAT results. I went back home to take the test results to my parents. As soon as I walked in, I saw my mother looking at my results. My mother turned and looked at me and told me I got a 1210 and that I made it. I walked into my bedroom and jumped on my bed and thanked the Lord for answering my prayers. Now I know that I can accomplish my goals and dreams and I can continue to follow my dreams. That is how the Lord has impacted my life.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

Answer: I am 35 years old, my hometown is Orlando Florida. I enjoy family activities and relaxing. My activities include reading and sports. My family makes me happy and one day I want to live in the country. I want kids one day and a house with a yard. I work too much and sometimes I get a little depressed but my family is wonderful and it makes me happy.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

Answer: I'm 52 years old. Grew up in Michigan with 3 brothers and 1 sister. My dad was a school principal and my mother was a stay-at-home mom. Great parents, very old-fashioned. Attended St Joe’s Catholic grade school and high school, before attending SIU in Carbondale IL. I graduated in 1985 and have been selling insurance and other financial products since 1991. As a successful business owner for over 25 years, I have a strong track record of creating business solutions, cutting costs and consistently increasing the performance and productivity of the department and individuals. I've never been afraid to ask questions and do the hard work necessary to get the job done. I believe if you truly give it your all, it makes the reward that much sweeter. I take pride in the relationships that I build. I can call myself a better person because of my faith and actions in life.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

Answer: My name is Sarah Burke. I am 23 years old and I grew up in the United States. My father is originally from Michigan and has been a web designer and programmer. I spent 18 years on the sidelines of plenty of IT lessons, and I have also dabbled in my own coding over the years. I was home-schooled, but I took most of my classes at a college with a high school curriculum. I took a lot of math classes and also have the ability to speak Chinese. I enjoy playing sports like basketball, golf and bowling. I’m an avid reader, although it has been a long time since I’ve had much time to pick up a book. I don’t have much free time, though, so I mostly make the most of what little time I have.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.

Answer: Sure! I’m 23 years old and grew up in Omaha, Nebraska. I went to public school K-12. In high school, I joined the debating team, the Academic Decathlon team, and the acting club. I believe this experience helped improve my communication and time management skills, as well as increase my confidence and general self-esteem. I went to UNO and earned my degree in accounting. I then joined AmeriCorps, which exposed me to real-life hands on teaching. I interned in the 1st and 3rd grade. I was given an opportunity to run an after-school program to prepare students for state tests. Eventually I was hired as the afterschool program director. Because I ran the program, I was able to come up with ideas on how to motivate students. One event I developed was a live game show at the end of the school year. I thought this would be good for my students as they always loved watching game shows. It really worked well, and I had a lot of fun. In addition, I had the opportunity to plan a vacation for 50 students to get away for a few days. It was just fun to see them have such a good time and build bonds that probably wouldn’t have happened otherwise.

I also got the chance to see how schools are affected by budget cuts. Some of the best teaching resources were axed, and the principal was going out of his way to buy things for his students. This inspired me to work in education even more. It also inspired me to figure out a way to help students succeed and still be able to do great things in my life.

So when I applied for Teach for America, I wanted to develop new tools and knowledge to reach and teach students. As an educator with TFA, I have learned many new skills that are essential to being an effective teacher. I am able to use those skills to impact my students. I have a better understanding of different educational and learning styles. My grammar is much better, and I have gained a better understanding of the teacher’s perspective in a classroom.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.

Answer: I am 32 years old. I spent the first 11 years of my life on a farm on the U.P. Michigan, where I helped raise cattle and collect eggs from chickens. When I turned 12 we moved to a more populated area. A year later we bought a dog at an auction. I ended up raising two dogs over 17 years. My parents divorced, and as I got older I moved out with my mom. In the city I studied studio art at a community college in 2006. During this time I also began photographing people. After graduating in 2008 I took a road trip down to Texas and then to California with my mom and our dogs. During this trip I was offered the job of being a photographer’s assistant. I moved to Cali to live with family. I studied Art History at a University for a year. I worked as a visual merchandiser at a department store for a year. I also became close to the M.F.A. program at Cal State Fullerton, where I became a part-time M.F.A. student in 2013. In September 2015, I began my Masters in M.F.A. in Photography at Cal State Fullerton. As a student I’ve gained a lot from visiting places like the Los Angeles Center for Photography and the Pasadena Center for the Photography in this area. I have also had a lot of hands on experience from hanging the M.F.A. shows. After I have finished my thesis project, I intend to return to California to finish my thesis show and become an assistant, working on a portrait project in California.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.

Answer: I’m 35, born and raised in southern Illinois, in the little town of Woodlawn, population 1,000 people. What is going on the news, and in the world today. I live in Ohio now, but came from Illinois, I also have family there. I recently went down there because I worked the Illinois primary on the Democratic side. But I went down there just to help them because I know they’re hurting economically, there’s loss of jobs, a lot of people lost their jobs. Trump lost by like 20-something thousand votes, it could have been different if we could get people motivated. I feel that we lose the black vote because they just don’t feel that we are getting anything out of the democratic party, and maybe they feel that maybe we have to rethink our strategy. But the Democratic Party had nothing to do with what happened in Illinois, because they hurt them economically. It was a Republican governor and a Republican president. What I do for my community is I just try to be an example of what a black man is supposed to be. I don’t make the perfect choices, but the choices that I make try to make it easier for my son and my daughter when they grow up to see a good man in a good relationship and a good family. I just want a good family, and I just want to be the best human being that I can be. I just try to serve and give back to the community as much as I can. I just want to try to save the community from these young black youths, that they’re in trouble now. We need to give them opportunities to let them feel like they’re important and like they’re loved.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.

Answer: Sir, I am a 39-year-old woman living in San Antonio, Texas. I grew up the second oldest of eight children in a small town located near the US-Mexico border in Laredo, Texas. I dropped out of high school at the age of 16 and eventually returned to graduate as the valedictorian of my high school class at the age of 19. I was encouraged by my eldest sister to attend Baylor University and I completed both my undergraduate and graduate studies there. My two year-old son accompanied me at most of my classes and was the recipient of the colleges most prestigious scholarship. I taught for five years at Baylor and taught writing at Saint Mary’s College as well.

In 2003, I moved back to Laredo with my son to live with my mother, who had suffered from an illness which made caring for her two young grandchildren an impossibility. We moved back to Texas because that was the only way I could be with my own children. Although I did not want to move back home, it was the right thing to do. I love my son more than I can express. I came back to San Antonio 3 years ago and have lived with my mother since. My mother, father, and my older sister have all passed away. I do not have any siblings here in San Antonio.

Currently, I care for my mother. I attend weekly therapy sessions which keep me calm and able to manage my responsibilities. I love my son. I am devastated that I will be in prison for such a long time. My life has not even begun.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.

Answer: I am 77 years old, born in the town of Balcarce, Argentina. I began a 45-year career in real estate brokerage and investing in the 1960’s with my husband, Dr. Saul Brenner, who was an obstetrician in the Chicago area. We expanded our real estate holding with the acquisition of apartment buildings, shopping centers, a professional office building, and retail strips centers. In the 1970’s, we began to invest in rental and owner-occupied apartments in Fort Lauderdale, Florida. We became very involved in our Jewish community in Chicago and Fort Lauderdale, including taking board and committee positions in many Jewish organizations. In 1996, Saul passed away; at that time, my now-16 year old granddaughter, Kalena, came to live with me. Over the next ten years, I became a caregiver to two of my grandchildren, Kalena and Oscar, who had been orphaned at birth. During that time, I was fortunate to participate in both undergraduate and graduate programs at the University of the District of Columbia in Washington, D.C., while developing my consulting business in organizational development and succession planning for Jewish not-for-profit organizations. When Kalena became of age to make decisions regarding her life, she decided to move to Israel, but I remained in the United States. I continue to own and manage my property in Ft. Lauderdale, Florida, and I devote a lot of time to my social justice work, which I see as my legacy in Saul’s memory. I am the founder of the Israeli – American Jewish Democratic Alliance (IAJDA). See www.IAJDA.org. I had previously founded, in 1982, the first Chicago – Tel Aviv Sister City Project, in conjunction with Mayor Jane Byrne.
